name: Korean Sesame Salt
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the salt substitute over vegetables or stews.
2. In a heavy skillet or wok, gradually heat the sesame seeds over low heat until golden and puffy. Shake the pan frequently to toast evenly.
3. Remove from heat when the sesame seeds begin to turn light brown and you can smell them.
4. Stir in the salt if using. Allow the mixture to cool.
5. Grind the sesame seeds in a blender or mortar.
6. Store in a tightly covered jar.
1 tsp, 17 calories, negligible fat exchange carbohydrate & protein & fiber, 1.5 grams of fat, 14 mg potassium, 18 mg sodium, 0 cholesterol.
